candidates.why: This appears to be a component of the selected parent row 'Cost of revenues/COGS'. The figures roll up into COGS together with 'Depreciation in COR/COGS' and 'Raw Material/Finished Goods', so adding it separately would double count.

This appears to be a component of the selected parent row 'Cost of revenues/COGS'. The figures roll up into COGS together with 'Amortization in COR/COGS' and 'Raw Material/Finished Goods', so adding it separately would double count.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is the total 'Cost of revenues/COGS'. The visible COR/COGS D&A rows are embedded components of that total, not separate same-level expenses to add later.

candidates.why: This is a separate same-level SG&A component alongside the selected XSGA rows 'Payroll Expenses' and 'Selling and Marketing Expense'. It is not embedded within those selected rows and should be added separately. It is also the summary row equal to 'Depreciation in SGA' plus 'Amortization in SGA', so it is preferred over components to avoid double counting.
This is a component of the summary row 'Depreciation/Amortization in SGA'. Do not select it when the summary row is present, to avoid double counting.
This is a component of the summary row 'Depreciation/Amortization in SGA'. Do not select it when the summary row is present, to avoid double counting.
This looks like a tiny balancing adjustment, not the main D&A row to add. The summary row 'Depreciation/Amortization in SGA' already captures the SG&A D&A amount.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA bucket consists of 'Payroll Expenses' and 'Selling and Marketing Expense', which do not include the separate SG&A D&A line. Use only the summary SG&A D&A row, not its components.
